title: P:EAR - 211info
text: P:EAR P:EAR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that offers arts and music, mentoring, educational, and outdoor recreational programs. Additional supportive services include bus tickets, haircuts, clothing, and support for pregnancy and parenting. Also offers meals and cooking classes. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and camping gear. 338 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 15-24 experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Friday: 9am-2pm Breakfast at 9am and lunch at noon to those served by p:ear Donations: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm Email (503) 228-6677 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 228-6677 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or walk in for more information. See website for donation information. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located near MAX yellow and green lines at the Union Station Stop, and one block from TriMet bus lines 4, 8, 44, and 77 Services: Bus Fare * Homeless Youth Camping Gear Donation Programs Clothing Donation Programs Community Meals * Homeless Youth Food Donation Programs General Clothing Provision * Homeless Youth General Recreational Activities/Sports * Homeless Youth Hair and Nail Care * Homeless Youth Homeless Drop In Centers * At Risk Youth Mentoring Programs * Homeless Youth Teen Expectant/New Parent Assistance * Homeless Youth If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. P:EAR
